T4_omega.test: Omega (no hardcoded constants; ratios vs within-run ctrl mean)

T5_tautology.note: Any non-Quran outlier passing at >=75% implies the test is tautological for multivariate outliers.

T8_path.test: T8 Path minimality + adjacent diversity (canonical order vs perms)

T9_markov.test: T9 Markov unforgeability (bigram root LL, canon vs shuffle)

T11_bigram_suf.test: T11 Bigram sufficiency H_3/H_2 (lower is more Markov-2)

T13_meccan_medinan.test: T13 Meccan vs Medinan H-Cascade (pre-reg test B)

T14_bootstrap_omega.test: T14 Bootstrap Omega stability (pre-reg test C)

T15_classifier.test: T15 5-fold CV logistic classifier AUC (Quran vs Arabic ctrl)

T16_scale_free.test: T16 Scale-Free S24 (EL+CN transitions, sliding window)
T16_scale_free.note: Simplified S24 (no semantic). Quran should show Fisher p < 0.01 at every window size if claim holds; controls should not.

T17_multi_scale_pert.note: mean_gap > 0 ⇔ canonical is FARTHER from control centroid than perturbed. Paper's structural-singularity signal.

T18_verse_internal.note: If word order within verse is NON-random and structural, shuffling should MOVE the unit toward the control cloud (mean_gap > 0) in the Quran but not in negatives.

T19_rqa.test: T19 RQA Laminarity on verse-length sequence
T19_rqa.note: Low LAM = anti-metric (no flat regions). Paper claims Quran LAM is lower than controls, d = -0.395.

T20_forgery_p3.note: Paper: Quran 93% collapse (Phi drops) vs controls 36%. Tests whether Quran's structure is more-than-rhyme.

T21_cross_lang.test: T21 Iliad self-test on lang-agnostic 5-D (CL1 partial)
T21_cross_lang.note: Only Iliad in repo; no Tanakh for full 3-way comparison. Compare to Quran Arabic-native numbers separately.

T23_harakat.note: H(h|r) measures residual uncertainty of diacritics given their consonant. Lower = harakat is structured by rasm. Near-maximum would mean random assignment.

T29_phi_frac.note: v10.15 audit downgrades this: scalar near 1/phi is a motif, NOT a certified critical point (see T30).

T30_rg_flow.note: alpha ~ 1 = trivial (independent blocks). alpha << 1 would indicate scale-invariance / criticality.

T31_fisher_curvature.note: curvature rank 1 = smoothest (low 1/lambda). The v10.12 finding was that Quran sits at the smooth end. This is a distinctive-but-not-unique signature.

T32_f6_length_coherence.test: T32 F6 length coherence (Spearman rho adj word-counts, Band A)
T32_f6_length_coherence.definition: F6(unit) = Spearman rho(len_i, len_{i+1}) over verses
T32_f6_length_coherence.nan_rate_note: F6 is nan when a unit has <4 verses or constant word-counts across adjacent pairs; ~56% of Band-A controls have nan, 0% of Quran.
T32_f6_length_coherence.relabel_from_master_report: MASTER_FINDINGS_REPORT called this 'anti-length-regularity' with d=-0.670. Direct measurement yields d=+0.88 (length COHERENCE, sign inverted). Property is real; interpretation corrected.

T33_veru.definition: VERU(unit) = unique verse-ending roots / n verses
T33_veru.verdict: FALSIFIED: controls have HIGHER VERU than Quran (d<0). Poetry's form constraints enforce rhyme-word uniqueness; the Quran does not. Adiyat canonical VERU=1.000 is achieved by ~27% of Quran-short and ~62% of ctrl-short units — not a distinctive signature. This corrects the old ADIYAT_CASE.md 'Law IV' claim.

T34_letter_swap.definition: For each variant of Adiyat v1, compute |delta|/sigma_ctrl per canonical feature. Sum of squares gives total Mahalanobis-like displacement in control-sigma units.
T34_letter_swap.honest_ceiling_note: Only 2 of 5 features (H_cond, T) move under single-letter swap. EL, VL_CV, CN are mathematically invariant because: EL depends on verse-final letter (unchanged when swap preserves final 'ا'), VL_CV depends on word counts (unchanged), CN depends on first word (unchanged). Max total displacement ~0.6 control-sigma — this is the true upper bound of 5-D letter-level detection.

T35_hurst_applicability.test: T35 Multi-scale Hurst applicability at Band-A
T35_hurst_applicability.definition: R/S Hurst requires N>=32 for 4 windows at chunk=8 and 2+ chunk sizes for log-log regression.
T35_hurst_applicability.verdict: PRACTICAL NULL: >90% of Band-A Arabic control units cannot support Hurst R/S estimation. The 'multi-level Hurst H=0.90 / H=0.81' figures from MASTER_FINDINGS_REPORT (based on the whole-Quran concatenated 6,236-verse sequence) do not generalise to per-surah-level features. Hurst is NOT a practical feature for a 5-D or higher Phi_M at Band-A or short-band scale.
